nodes.title: Fisheries Management
nodes.description: Principles of fisheries management, including population assessment, analytical methods, harvest allocation models, human dimensions, policy and emerging issues. Prerequisites: BIOL 1000, BIOL 1400, or BCOR 1400; BIOL 1450 or BCOR 1450; WFB 3610.
nodes.other_requirements: Students are expected to apply the skills learned in WFB 2170 (Scientific Writing) in all submitted assignments.

nodes.title: SL:Applied Land Manag-Wildlif
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.

nodes.title: Methods Fisheries and Wildlife
nodes.description: Familiarizes students with early-stage wildlife and fisheries biology techniques that are commonly used for both data collection and analysis; takes a multi-taxa approach to highlight key similarities and differences in habitat and life history that may influence how research and management studies are designed and conducted. Prerequisites: Wildlife and Fisheries Biology major; minimum Sophomore standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Wildlife and Fisheries Biology major; minimum Sophomore standing.

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required and completion of an Experiential Learning plan required.

nodes.title: Wetlands Wildlife Ecology
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: Having some knowledge of biology, marine ecosystems, oceanography and/or basic ecology will be beneficial although there are no specific prerequisites.

nodes.title: Accelerated Biology
nodes.description: Selected topics from the full year of introductory biology, compressed into one semester. For students with demonstrated mastery of basic biology (e.g., AP credit). Permission required. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1425 and BCOR 1400 or BIOL 1400. Pre/co-requisite: CHEM 1400, CHEM 1405, or CHEM 1410.
nodes.other_requirements: For students with demonstrated mastery of basic biology (e.g., AP credit). Permission required. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1425 and BCOR 1400 or BIOL 1400. By invitation only; for FTFY students with AP score of 4. Instructor permission required. Must register for a BCOR 1425 lab. Must be enrolled in CHEM 1400 if have no CHEM credit.

nodes.title: Wildlife & Fisheries Honors
nodes.description: Honors course.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Learn to Hunt
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific title.

nodes.title: Fisheries Biology & Techniques
nodes.description: Introduction to freshwater fish, habitats, and life histories. Overview of fishery techniques, including sampling and assessment methods, stocking, harvest regulations, population and habitat evaluation. Prerequisites: BIOL 1000, BIOL 1400, or BCOR 1400; BIOL 1005, BCOR 1425, BIOL 1450, or BCOR 1450.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Junior standing; Lab will be subset into shorter times for smaller groups as needed for boat and lab work; Course fee $129.00; System waitlisting enabled for this section.

nodes.title: Wildlife Behavior
nodes.description: Behavior and social organization of game and nongame species as they pertain to population management. Prerequisites: BIOL 1400, BCOR 1400, or BIOL 1000; BIOL 1450 or BCOR 1450; NR 2030 or BCOR 2100.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Principles of Biology 2
nodes.description: Principles of organismal biology; nature of scientific inquiry, plant form and function, pollination ecology, animal phylogeny illustrated by comparative anatomy and physiology; animal behavior. Credit not awarded for both BIOL 1450 and BCOR 1450.
nodes.other_requirements: ASCI, BIOL, BISC, MGEN, MICR, NEUR, PBIO, ZOOL majors should register for BCOR 1450; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; Post Bac Pre med students register for BIOL 1450

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Application and Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Ornithology
nodes.description: Taxonomy, classification, identification, morphology, physiology, behavior, and ecology of birds. Prerequisites: BIOL 1000, BIOL 1400, or BCOR 1400; BIOL 1005, BCOR 1425, BIOL 1450, or BCOR 1450.
nodes.other_requirements: Prereqs enforced by the system: (BIOL 1400ORBCOR 1400ORBIOL 1000) AND (BIOL 1450ORBCOR 1450ORBIOL 1000) ORBCOR 1425 Ornithology (WFB 130) is designed to give students an in-depth introduction to avian ecology. The course covers the evolution, morphology, physiology, behavior, ecology, conservation, and identification of common birds of Vermont. The course content is designed to give students a deeper understanding of the life of birds and an appreciation of their diversity and natural history. The course is primarily lecture-based but students will have an opportunity to explore birds in the field through an on-line field-journal assignment. Note that this assignment will require students to go outdoors and observe birds/bird behavior outside of the class meeting pattern. The class is in-person. Students who miss class are encouraged to check-in with peers or schedule time with me or the TAs to review anything that is not clear from lectures.

nodes.title: Conservation Biology
nodes.description: Conservation of biological diversity at genetic, species, ecosystem, and landscape levels. Emphasis on genetic diversity, population viability, endangered species, critical habitats, international implications. Discussion section covers basic genetic principles, population genetics, and population modeling. Prerequisites: BIOL 1000, BIOL 1400, or BCOR 1400; BIOL 1005, BCOR 1425, BIOL 1450, or BCOR 1450.
nodes.other_requirements: Must register for a Lab WFB 3240: A01,A02,A03;

nodes.title: Wildlife & Fisheries Honors
nodes.description: Honors project dealing with wildlife or fisheries biology.
nodes.other_requirements: Permission of Program Chair required; WFB majors only

nodes.title: Principles of Biology 1
nodes.description: Principles of cellular biochemistry; cell biology; genetics and evolution. Topics: biochemistry; metabolism, cell structure/function; respiration; photosynthesis; molecular, Mendelian and population genetics; genetics of evolution. Credit not awarded for both BIOL 1400 and BCOR 1400 or BCOR 1425.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not awarded for both BIOL 1400 and BCOR 1400 or BCOR 1425. Must register for a BIOL 1400 lab; Lab supply fee: $30; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; Post Bac Pre Med & PACE students register for BIOL 1400 ZRA Colocated with BIOL 1400 ZRA

nodes.title: Wildlife Conservation
nodes.description: Historical and contemporary values of wildlife; impacts on habitats and populations; strategies for conservation, allocation, and use. Nonmajors only.

nodes.title: Ecology, Ecosystems & Environ
nodes.description: Major ecological concepts and their application. Analysis of form, structure, and function of organisms, populations, communities, ecosystems, and landscapes. Prerequisites: RSENR students or Agroecology or Landscape Design or Environmental Studies majors; minimum Sophomore standing.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Sophomore standing

nodes.title: Field Herpetology
nodes.description: Introduction to the identification, life histories, habitats, conservation, and field study of Vermont's reptiles and amphibians.

nodes.title: Scientific Writing and Interpr
nodes.description: Focus on effective communication within the genre of scientific research by focusing on technical writing, revising and editing, interpreting data, creating figures and tables, critically reading and data mining the literature, and producing an original scientific research manuscript. Prerequisites: BIOL 1000 or BIOL 1400 or BCOR 1400; Wildlife and Fisheries Biology majors; Minimum Sophomore standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Wildlife and Fisheries Biology majors; Minimum Sophomore standing. WFB Majors only.

nodes.title: Terrestrial Wildlife Ecology
nodes.description: Wildlife ecology with an emphasis on the management and conservation of species, populations, and ecosystems. Prerequisites: WFB 2740; NR 2030 or BCOR 2100.

nodes.title: Prin of Wildlife Management
nodes.description: Application of ecology and sociology to the management of wildlife populations and habitat; integration of wildlife management with demands for other resources; consideration of game species, endangered species, and biological diversity. Prerequisite: NR 2030 or BCOR 2100.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small research projects under the supervision of a faculty member for which credit is awarded. Formal report required. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum junior standing; Permission required (must submit project prospectus)

nodes.title: Ichthyology
nodes.description: Biology of fishes. Focus is on form and function, morphology, physiology, behavior, life history, and ecology of modern fishes. Prerequisites: BIOL 1000, BIOL 1400, or BCOR 1400; BIOL 1005, BCOR 1425, BIOL 1450, or BCOR 1450; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Junior standing

nodes.title: Herpetology
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.

nodes.title: Human Wildlife Coexistence
nodes.description: Explores the challenges and opportunities of human-wildlife coexistence across ecological, cultural, ethical, and political dimensions. Students will engage with key theories, real-world case studies, creative reflections, and practitioner insights to better understand how humans and wildlife share space in today's changing world. Emphasizing sustainability, global citizenship, and the diversity of human experience, the course highlights cultural values, ethical complexities, and community contexts that shape coexistence practices around the world. Prerequisite: Minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Junior standing.

nodes.title: Exploring Biology 2
nodes.description: An evolutionary perspective to exploring biology. Topics include: patterns of inheritance, Darwinian evolution, evolution of biodiversity, ecology of organisms, human effects on biological systems. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1450 and BIOL 1450.

nodes.title: Ecology and Evolution
nodes.description: Ecosystem and community structure, population growth, species interactions and niche dynamics, population and chromosomal genetics, speciation in fossil records, ecology of animal behavior, applied ecology. Prerequisites: BCOR 1400, BIOL 1400, or BIOL 1000; BCOR 1450, BIOL 1450, BIOL 1005, or BCOR 1425; MATH 1212 or MATH 1234.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override.

nodes.title: Exploring Biology 1
nodes.description: Exploring biology from cells to organisms. Topics include origins of life, ancestral organisms, uni- and multi- cellular energetics, evolution of respiration and metabolism, and the genetic code. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1400 and BIOL 1400 or BCOR 1425.

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Junior standing; Permission required (must submit project prospectus)

nodes.title: Internship
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required
